# Routing Requirements Checklist: Game View Transition

**Purpose**: Validate routing/redirect requirements quality — completeness, clarity, consistency, measurability, and coverage across spec, plan, and tasks
**Created**: 2026-06-11
**Feature**: specs/005-fix-game-view/spec.md

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are redirect requirements defined for ALL room status transitions (lobby→playing→finished) across ALL affected pages (LobbyPage, GamePage, JoinRoomPage)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001–FR-008]
- [ ] CHK002 Are the participant's role display requirements defined for both "drawer" and "guesser" roles on the game page? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK003 Is the poll-failure error behavior (non-blocking indicator with silent retry) captured as a functional requirement, or only mentioned in Edge Cases? [Gap, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK004 Are requirements defined for the "finished" state distinction between navigating to /game (redirect) vs transitioning while on the page (show message)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK005 Are requirements defined for the missing-session scenario with specific redirect behavior, not just "redirect to join page"? [Completeness, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is "sub-3-second intervals" in FR-002 sufficiently precise, or should a fixed value (e.g., 2 seconds) be explicitly stated? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK007 Is "within 3 seconds" in SC-001 clearly bounded (e.g., wall-clock time inclusive of poll cycle + network latency vs exclusive)? [Clarity, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "appropriate message" in the missing-session edge case defined with specific content, or left to implementer discretion? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK009 Is "clear rejection message" in SC-004 specified with required wording or format? [Clarity, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK010 Is "non-blocking error indicator" for poll failure described with enough visual/behavioral specificity (position, duration, dismissal)? [Clarity, Spec §Edge Cases]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Does the direct-navigation edge case ("otherwise redirect to join page") align with FR-006's distinct redirect targets for lobby (→/lobby) vs finished (→/) states? [Consistency, Spec §Edge Cases vs §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK012 Do the acceptance scenarios for US2 Scenario 1 match the clarification that this applies only to returning participants with existing sessions (not new joiners)? [Consistency, Spec §US2]
- [ ] CHK013 Are redirect actions consistent between the Page Routing by Status table (data-model.md) and the Functional Requirements (spec.md)? [Consistency]
- [ ] CHK014 Is "participant" used consistently throughout all artifacts, or does "player" appear as a synonym in SC-004? [Consistency, Spec §SC-004 vs §FR-001–FR-008]
- [ ] CHK015 Is the "first render cycle" constraint in SC-002 consistent with the mount-time vs poll-time distinction across US1 and US2 tasks? [Consistency, Spec §SC-002 vs tasks.md]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Can SC-001 (3-second redirect from host click) be objectively verified during manual testing given network latency variability? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK017 Is SC-002 (role on first render cycle) testable without instrumenting the React render cycle, or does it need a relaxed criterion? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK018 Is SC-003 (100% of refresh participants redirected) quantified with a specific sample size or test methodology? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK019 Are the acceptance scenarios for US1 Given/When/Then statements independently verifiable without knowledge of the other story's behavior? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §US1]
- [ ] CHK020 Are the acceptance scenarios for US2 independently testable from US1, given they share the same LobbyPage component? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §US2]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK021 Are requirements defined for a participant navigating to /game while the room is in "lobby" state? [Coverage, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK022 Are requirements defined for a participant on the game page when the status transitions to "finished" via polling? [Coverage, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK023 Are requirements defined for a returning participant (existing session) who navigates to /lobby while the game is already in "playing" state? [Coverage, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK024 Are requirements defined for a participant who joins a room that transitions to "playing" before their first poll completes? [Coverage, Gap]
- [ ] CHK025 Are requirements defined for the host's post-start experience (still on lobby? auto-redirect?) separate from other participants? [Coverage, Spec §US1]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK026 Are requirements defined for concurrent status transitions, e.g., host starts game while another participant's poll request is in-flight (race condition)? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [ ] CHK027 Are requirements defined for rapid status transitions, e.g., game starts and ends (drawer disconnects) before the next poll cycle completes? [Edge Case, Gap]
- [ ] CHK028 Is the "Game already in progress" error message format (text, placement, styling) specified for the JoinRoomPage UI? [Coverage, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK029 Are requirements defined for a participant on the game page during "playing" whose session expires mid-game? [Edge Case, Gap]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK030 Is the assumption "participant identity and room identifier are persisted in browser session" explicitly validated or treated as a documented risk? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK031 Is the dependency on "game page already handles role-based rendering per game rounds spec" explicitly called out as a prerequisite? [Dependency, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK032 Is the polling interval (2 seconds) explicitly stated in the spec, or only referenced via "sub-3-second" in FR-002 and "2s" in plan.md? [Clarity, Spec §FR-002 vs plan.md]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
- Focus: Routing + error/edge case + UX states (full scope)
- Depth: Standard PR review gate
